lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 201: Revision after feedback under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Revision after feedback
lessons.englishTrap: C1 growth comes from second drafts, not just first attempts.
lessons.spanishLogic: Revise a draft based on criticism: clarify, soften, reorder, and strengthen.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: La versión revisada reduce la ambigüedad y mejora la transición final.
Además, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

controlled.title: Model & structure
controlled.instructions: Draft short models and label the function of each sentence.
prompts: [201 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Revision after feedback about a difficult decision in a family context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

[201 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Revision after feedback about a delayed trip in a travel context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

[201 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Revision after feedback about a change in routine in a health context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

[201 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Revision after feedback about a rent increase in a housing context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

[201 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Revision after feedback about an exam result in a study context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.
answers: Model direction: La versión revisada reduce la ambigüedad y mejora la transición final. Add one supporting sentence and one nuanced consequence. Functions must be explicit. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

mutation.title: Revision workshop
mutation.instructions: Rewrite weak drafts into precise, cohesive C1 Spanish.
prompts: [201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a family context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Pienso que esto es muy bueno y muy importante porque ayuda a la gente y tiene muchas cosas positivas.

[201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a travel context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Estoy escribiendo para decir que tengo un problema y quiero que ustedes lo arreglen pronto porque no es bueno.

[201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a health context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Hay dos opiniones y las dos son interesantes pero mi opinión es que una es mejor porque sí.

[201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a housing context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: El gráfico sube y baja y eso significa que la situación es buena para todos.

[201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a study context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Fue una experiencia mala y aprendí muchas cosas importantes sobre la vida.

[201 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a money context using Revision after feedback. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: La solución es hacer más comunicación y tener mejor organización en el futuro.
answers: Good revision should: remove vague words, add a concrete relation between ideas, use controlled register, and include a connector such as 'además'. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

contrast.title: Rhetorical diagnosis
contrast.instructions: Critique weak paragraph plans and replace them with stronger structures.
prompts: [201 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a difficult decision: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Revision after feedback.

[201 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a delayed trip: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Revision after feedback.

[201 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a change in routine: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Revision after feedback.

[201 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a rent increase: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Revision after feedback.

[201 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for an exam result: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Revision after feedback.
answers: Expected critique: missing context, support/evidence, concession, and implication. Better structure: context/problem → claim → support/example → concession or recommendation. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

reverse.title: C1 transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate/adapt complex English meaning into natural Spanish without calquing syntax.
prompts: [201 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a job offer may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[201 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although an inconsistent habit may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[201 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a defective product may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[201 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a missing document may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[201 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although an unexpected fee may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.
answers: Possible answer: Aunque una oferta de trabajo parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un hábito irregular parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un producto defectuoso parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un documento pendiente parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un cargo inesperado parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use claim → support → concession → implication.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 200: Narrative used as evidence under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Narrative used as evidence
lessons.englishTrap: Anecdotes need interpretation.
lessons.spanishLogic: Use a short story to support a claim without letting the story take over.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Esa experiencia ilustra por qué la claridad inicial evita conflictos posteriores.
Por eso, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 196: Expansion: develop an underbuilt idea under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Expansion: develop an underbuilt idea
lessons.englishTrap: Learners often state true things without developing them.
lessons.spanishLogic: Turn a thin claim into a supported paragraph with example and implication.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: La práctica constante ayuda, pero solo si se convierte en retroalimentación útil.
Aun así, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 194: Hypothesis and speculation under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Hypothesis and speculation
lessons.englishTrap: Speculation must sound controlled, not random.
lessons.spanishLogic: Use conditional, future of probability, and compound subjunctive for plausible interpretations.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Es posible que la decisión se haya tomado con información incompleta.
A medida que, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

reverse.title: C1 transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate/adapt complex English meaning into natural Spanish without calquing syntax.
prompts: [193 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a delayed trip may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[193 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a change in routine may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[193 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a rent increase may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[193 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although an exam result may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[193 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a tight budget may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.
answers: Possible answer: Aunque un viaje retrasado parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un cambio de rutina parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque una subida del alquiler parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un resultado de examen parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un presupuesto ajustado parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.mistake: Writing a list of opinions instead of an argument.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: The paragraph has no rhetorical spine.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use claim → support → concession → implication.

commonErrors.mistake: Overclaiming with siempre, todos, nunca, obviamente.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: English emphasis replaces Spanish precision.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use tiende a, puede, no necesariamente, en parte, hasta cierto punto.

commonErrors.mistake: Making formal writing sound like chat.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Register was not planned before drafting.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Choose audience first, then pronouns, verbs, openings, and level of directness.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: No revision pass for reference chains.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use esta medida, dicho enfoque, lo anterior, este problema, esa posibilidad.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 192: Complex causal explanation under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Complex causal explanation
lessons.englishTrap: Because/so is too flat for C1 explanations.
lessons.spanishLogic: Show multiple causes, mediating factors, and consequences.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: La demora no se debió a un solo factor, sino a una combinación de fallos.
Por otra parte, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

controlled.title: Model & structure
controlled.instructions: Draft short models and label the function of each sentence.
prompts: [192 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Complex causal explanation about a defective product in a shopping context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

[192 · Model analysis] Draft a 3-sentence Spanish model for Complex causal explanation about a missing document in a immigration context. Label each sentence function in English: claim/context, support, nuance/consequence.

Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

mutation.title: Revision workshop
mutation.instructions: Rewrite weak drafts into precise, cohesive C1 Spanish.
prompts: [192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a shopping context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Pienso que esto es muy bueno y muy importante porque ayuda a la gente y tiene muchas cosas positivas.

[192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a immigration context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Estoy escribiendo para decir que tengo un problema y quiero que ustedes lo arreglen pronto porque no es bueno.

[192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a banking context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Hay dos opiniones y las dos son interesantes pero mi opinión es que una es mejor porque sí.

[192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a restaurant context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: El gráfico sube y baja y eso significa que la situación es buena para todos.

[192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a transport context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: Fue una experiencia mala y aprendí muchas cosas importantes sobre la vida.

[192 · Revision] Rewrite this weak draft into C1-level Spanish for a education context using Complex causal explanation. Keep the meaning but improve precision, cohesion, and register: La solución es hacer más comunicación y tener mejor organización en el futuro.
answers: Good revision should: remove vague words, add a concrete relation between ideas, use controlled register, and include a connector such as 'por otra parte'. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

contrast.title: Rhetorical diagnosis
contrast.instructions: Critique weak paragraph plans and replace them with stronger structures.
prompts: [192 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a defective product: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Complex causal explanation.

[192 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for an unreliable route: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Complex causal explanation.
answers: Expected critique: missing context, support/evidence, concession, and implication. Better structure: context/problem → claim → support/example → concession or recommendation. lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 191: Cohesion with reference chains under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Cohesion with reference chains
lessons.englishTrap: Repeating nouns creates learner-like writing.
lessons.spanishLogic: Use this/that/above-mentioned ideas naturally: esta medida, lo anterior, dicho enfoque.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Dicho enfoque no resolvería todo, pero sí reduciría el riesgo inicial.
Por una parte, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 185: Data/trend interpretation under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Data/trend interpretation
lessons.englishTrap: C1 commentary distinguishes what the data shows from what it might imply.
lessons.spanishLogic: Describe changes cautiously and avoid overclaiming from limited evidence.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Los datos apuntan a una mejora moderada, aunque la muestra sigue siendo limitada.
Por eso, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 183: Complaint with evidence and remedy under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Complaint with evidence and remedy
lessons.englishTrap: Being polite is not the same as being vague.
lessons.spanishLogic: Explain the issue, attach evidence, request a specific correction, and preserve tone.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Solicito que revisen el cargo y me indiquen el procedimiento para corregirlo.

No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 179: Synthesis of two viewpoints under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Synthesis of two viewpoints
lessons.englishTrap: Do not summarize A, then B, then abruptly state your opinion.
lessons.spanishLogic: Combine two positions and explain their relationship before giving your conclusion.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Ambas posturas coinciden en el problema, pero discrepan sobre el método.
A medida que, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Performance 177: Mini-essay argument with thesis and implication under constraints
lessons.targetPattern: Mini-essay argument with thesis and implication
lessons.englishTrap: Listing opinions is not C1; the reader must see the logic.
lessons.spanishLogic: Build a compact argument: thesis, reason, evidence, concession, implication.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: A mi juicio, la medida es razonable porque reduce la incertidumbre inicial.
Por otra parte, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 170: Compression: say more with fewer words

lessons.englishTrap: Long does not mean advanced.
lessons.spanishLogic: Remove filler, redundancy, and vague intensifiers while keeping nuance.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing

Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm

La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 168: Complex concession and limitation
lessons.targetPattern: Complex concession and limitation
lessons.englishTrap: Nuance comes from holding tension without contradiction.
lessons.spanishLogic: Use si bien, por mucho que, aun cuando, pese a que, and eso no implica que.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing

Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre

No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 164: Diplomatic disagreement
lessons.targetPattern: Diplomatic disagreement
lessons.englishTrap: C1 disagreement is firm and socially controlled.
lessons.spanishLogic: Disagree clearly without sounding hostile or evasive.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Entiendo el argumento, pero no comparto del todo la conclusión.

La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 162: Reflective narrative with interpretation
lessons.targetPattern: Reflective narrative with interpretation
lessons.englishTrap: C1 narration includes evaluation, not just chronology.
lessons.spanishLogic: Narrate what happened, why it mattered, and what changed afterward.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Aquella conversación me obligó a replantearme cómo estaba estudiando.
Por otra parte, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.spanishLogic: Describe changes cautiously and avoid overclaiming from limited evidence.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion

Control register for audience and genre

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 155: Neutral summary of an article or opinion
lessons.targetPattern: Neutral summary of an article or opinion
lessons.englishTrap: C1 writing separates summary from evaluation.
lessons.spanishLogic: Report someone else's argument without copying or adding your own stance too early.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: El texto plantea que la flexibilidad mejora la productividad, aunque exige coordinación.
Por eso, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

lessons.title: Spanish C1 Composition 153: Counterargument and rebuttal
lessons.targetPattern: Counterargument and rebuttal
lessons.englishTrap: A weak essay ignores objections; a stronger one absorbs them.
lessons.spanishLogic: Represent an opposing view fairly, then limit or refute it.
formula: Plan the rhetorical function before writing
Use evidence/example, not just opinion
Add concession or limitation to avoid overclaiming
Control register for audience and genre
Revise for cohesion, precision, and sentence rhythm
naturalExamples: Es cierto que el coste inicial es alto; aun así, la inversión puede justificarse.
De hecho, conviene interpretar la situación con prudencia.
No se trata de escribir más, sino de organizar mejor la relación entre las ideas.
La conclusión debe añadir una consecuencia, no repetir la tesis.

prompts: [152 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a defective product: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Mini-essay argument with thesis and implication.

[152 · Rhetorical diagnosis] Write a short Spanish critique of this paragraph plan for a missing document: 'Sentence 1: my opinion. Sentence 2: another opinion. Sentence 3: conclusion.' Explain what is missing, then propose a better 4-sentence structure for Mini-essay argument with thesis and implication.

writing.title: Full C1 composition
writing.instructions: Open-ended C1 writing. The LLM grader should evaluate argument, register, cohesion, grammar, and revision quality.
prompts: [152 · C1 writing] Write 140-180 words in Spanish about an unreliable route in a transport situation. Genre/focus: Mini-essay argument with thesis and implication. Requirements: clear opening, developed reasoning, at least one concession, one precise connector chain, register appropriate to the audience, and a final sentence that adds implication rather than repetition.

reverse.title: C1 transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate/adapt complex English meaning into natural Spanish without calquing syntax.
prompts: [152 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a difficult decision may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[152 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a delayed trip may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[152 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a change in routine may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[152 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although a rent increase may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.

[152 · Transfer] Translate/adapt this into natural C1 Spanish: Although an exam result may look like a minor issue, it reveals a deeper problem: people are making decisions without enough context, so the next step should be cautious but concrete.
answers: Possible answer: Aunque una decisión difícil parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: Aunque un viaje retrasado parezca un asunto menor, revela un problema de fondo: se están tomando decisiones sin suficiente contexto; por eso, el siguiente paso debería ser prudente pero concreto. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.mistake: Writing a list of opinions instead of an argument.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: The paragraph has no rhetorical spine.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use claim → support → concession → implication.

commonErrors.mistake: Overclaiming with siempre, todos, nunca, obviamente.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: English emphasis replaces Spanish precision.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use tiende a, puede, no necesariamente, en parte, hasta cierto punto.

commonErrors.mistake: Making formal writing sound like chat.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Register was not planned before drafting.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Choose audience first, then pronouns, verbs, openings, and level of directness.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: No revision pass for reference chains.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use esta medida, dicho enfoque, lo anterior, este problema, esa posibilidad.

contrast.title: Error diagnosis
contrast.instructions: Find the problem, explain it briefly, then rewrite.
prompts: [151 · Diagnose] In this fitness example, identify at least two problems in the weak sentence and rewrite it. Weak: Creo que un hábito irregular es importante pero sin embargo la gente necesita hacer una decisión rápido.

reverse.title: Translation & transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate meaning naturally without copying English structure.
prompts: [151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the security situation, a small incident may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an analyst explains the limits clearly.

[151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the planning situation, a risky plan may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a coordinator explains the limits clearly.
[151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the work situation, a missed deadline may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a manager explains the limits clearly.

[151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the family situation, a difficult decision may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a relative explains the limits clearly.
[151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the travel situation, a delayed trip may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a passenger explains the limits clearly.

[151 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the health situation, a change in routine may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a doctor explains the limits clearly.
answers: Possible answer: En la situación de security, un incidente menor puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una analista explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de planning, un plan arriesgado puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un coordinador explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de work, un plazo incumplido puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un jefe explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de family, una decisión difícil puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un familiar explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de travel, un viaje retrasado puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un pasajero explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de health, un cambio de rutina puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una médica explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.correct_spanish: Add precision, soften overclaims, and connect ideas.

reverse.title: Translation & transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate meaning naturally without copying English structure.
prompts: [146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the immigration situation, a missing document may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an official explains the limits clearly.

[146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the banking situation, an unexpected fee may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an adviser explains the limits clearly.

[146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the restaurant situation, a poor service experience may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a waiter explains the limits clearly.

[146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the transport situation, an unreliable route may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a driver explains the limits clearly.

[146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the education situation, a confusing assignment may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a student explains the limits clearly.

[146 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the security situation, a small incident may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an analyst explains the limits clearly.
answers: Possible answer: En la situación de immigration, un documento pendiente puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una funcionaria explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de banking, un cargo inesperado puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un asesor explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de restaurant, una mala experiencia de servicio puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un camarero explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de transport, una ruta poco fiable puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una conductora explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de education, una tarea confusa puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que un estudiante explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de security, un incidente menor puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una analista explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.mistake: Choosing subjunctive/indicative by English translation only.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: English hides speaker stance.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Choose by function: fact, doubt, purpose, future condition, evaluation, or non-specific reference.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The logic is left implicit.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use connectors that match the relationship: sin embargo, por lo tanto, de hecho, no obstante.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The sentence has grammar but no register control.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Add precision, soften overclaims, and connect ideas.

lessons.targetPattern: Summarizing without copying
lessons.englishTrap: Copying phrases is not the same as summarizing.

formula: Notice the grammatical choice and name the function
Rewrite literal English into natural Spanish

naturalExamples: El autor sostiene que la flexibilidad mejora el rendimiento, aunque exige coordinación.
Dicho esto, conviene matizar la idea.

reverse.title: Translation & transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate meaning naturally without copying English structure.
prompts: [138 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the study situation, an exam result may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a teacher explains the limits clearly.
[138 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the money situation, a tight budget may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a client explains the limits clearly.

[138 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the technology situation, a confusing app may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a support team explains the limits clearly.

Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.mistake: Choosing subjunctive/indicative by English translation only.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: English hides speaker stance.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Choose by function: fact, doubt, purpose, future condition, evaluation, or non-specific reference.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The logic is left implicit.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use connectors that match the relationship: sin embargo, por lo tanto, de hecho, no obstante.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The sentence has grammar but no register control.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Add precision, soften overclaims, and connect ideas.

prompts: [102 · Diagnose] In this travel example, identify at least two problems in the weak sentence and rewrite it. Weak: Creo que un viaje retrasado es importante pero sin embargo la gente necesita hacer una decisión rápido.

writing.title: Paragraph task
writing.instructions: Open-ended paragraph writing. The LLM grader should judge clarity, grammar, register, and C1 trajectory.
prompts: [102 · Paragraph] Write 90-120 words in Spanish about an exam result in a study situation. Include: topic sentence, one concrete detail, Concession with aunque, si bien, aun cuando, one concession or limitation, and a final practical recommendation. Avoid translating English sentence order.

[102 · Paragraph] Write 90-120 words in Spanish about a tight budget in a money situation. Include: topic sentence, one concrete detail, Concession with aunque, si bien, aun cuando, one concession or limitation, and a final practical recommendation. Avoid translating English sentence order.

[102 · Paragraph] Write 90-120 words in Spanish about a tense conversation in a friendship situation. Include: topic sentence, one concrete detail, Concession with aunque, si bien, aun cuando, one concession or limitation, and a final practical recommendation. Avoid translating English sentence order.

[102 · Paragraph] Write 90-120 words in Spanish about a confusing app in a technology situation. Include: topic sentence, one concrete detail, Concession with aunque, si bien, aun cuando, one concession or limitation, and a final practical recommendation. Avoid translating English sentence order.

[102 · Paragraph] Write 90-120 words in Spanish about a neighborhood problem in a community situation. Include: topic sentence, one concrete detail, Concession with aunque, si bien, aun cuando, one concession or limitation, and a final practical recommendation. Avoid translating English sentence order.

reverse.title: Translation & transfer
reverse.instructions: Translate meaning naturally without copying English structure.
prompts: [102 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the community situation, a neighborhood problem may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a neighbor explains the limits clearly.
[102 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the career situation, a job offer may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a recruiter explains the limits clearly.

[102 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the fitness situation, an inconsistent habit may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless a coach explains the limits clearly.

[102 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the immigration situation, a missing document may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an official explains the limits clearly.

[102 · Transfer] Translate into natural Spanish, not word-for-word: In the banking situation, an unexpected fee may seem simple, but it will not be useful unless an adviser explains the limits clearly.
answers: Possible answer: En la situación de community, un problema del barrio puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una vecina explique los límites con claridad. Possible answer: En la situación de shopping, un producto defectuoso puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una vendedora explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

Possible answer: En la situación de immigration, un documento pendiente puede parecer sencillo, pero no será útil a menos que una funcionaria explique los límites con claridad. Rubric: natural Spanish, target structure used correctly, clear logic, appropriate register, and no literal English syntax.

commonErrors.mistake: Choosing subjunctive/indicative by English translation only.
commonErrors.why_it_happens: English hides speaker stance.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Choose by function: fact, doubt, purpose, future condition, evaluation, or non-specific reference.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The logic is left implicit.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Use connectors that match the relationship: sin embargo, por lo tanto, de hecho, no obstante.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: The sentence has grammar but no register control.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Add precision, soften overclaims, and connect ideas.

lessons.englishTrap: English because/so becomes repetitive and flat.
lessons.spanishLogic: Link causes and consequences across clauses without repeating porque.
formula: Notice the grammatical choice and name the function

naturalExamples: El plazo se redujo; de ahí que tuviéramos que priorizar.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 81: Reported Speech and Tense Backshift
lessons.targetPattern: Convert direct speech to indirect with que (or si/cuándo for questions); backshift tenses after past reporting verbs: present→imperfect, future→conditional, present perfect→past perfect, imperative→imperfect subjunctive; adjust time words when needed.
lessons.englishTrap: Dropping que, keeping original tenses after said/told, and translating questions with English word order.
lessons.spanishLogic: Indirect speech frames the original message inside a reporting verb. Past reporting usually backshifts tense, commands become imperfect subjunctive, and yes/no questions use si while wh-questions keep the question word.
formula: statement: X dijo/dijo que + backshifted clause
present reporting: Maria dice que quiere... (no shift)
present → imperfect: Estoy cansado → dijo que estaba cansado
future → conditional: Terminare pronto → dijo que terminaria pronto
imperative → imperfect subjunctive: Escribe → me dijo que escribiera
wh-question: pregunto cuando... / yes-no: pregunto si...
naturalExamples: Sofia dijo que estaba muy cansada hoy.
Carlos dijo que terminaria el informe pronto.
Marta dijo que habia hablado con su jefe.
Me dijo que escribiera mi nombre en la lista.
Miguel pregunto cuando empezaba la reunion.

Me dijo que escribiera mi nombre en la lista.

Carmen pregunto si habia estado en Madrid.

Luis dijo que llamaria al cliente al dia siguiente.
Ellos dijeron que enviaron la propuesta ayer.

La directora dijo que no compartieramos esto externamente.

mutation.instructions: Transform each base reported sentence as directed.
prompts: Base: Sofia dijo que estaba muy cansada hoy. -> change reporting verb to present: Sofia says...
Base: Carlos dijo que terminaria el informe pronto. -> change to direct quote with future.
Base: Ellos dijeron que fueron al concierto anoche. -> shift time expression to la noche anterior.
Base: Marta dijo que habia hablado con su jefe. -> use preterite backshift option instead of past perfect.
Base: Pablo dijo que cuando estudiaba en la universidad, trabajaba en un cafe. -> change subject to ella.
Base: Me dijo que escribiera mi nombre en la lista. -> change to negative command original: Don't write...
Base: La jefa dijo que no usaran los telefonos en la reunion. -> change to singular usted form command.
Base: Miguel pregunto cuando empezaba la pelicula. -> change to direct WH question.
Base: Carmen pregunto si habia estado en Madrid. -> change to direct yes/no question.
Base: Ana dijo que necesitaba el archivo hoy. -> change reporting to present (no backshift).
Base: Luis dijo que llamaria al cliente manana. -> change time to al dia siguiente.
Base: Elena dijo que habia revisado el contrato. -> change to present perfect in direct form first, then back again.
Base: Mi colega me dijo que le enviara la actualizacion antes del mediodia. -> change to affirmative command.
Base: El cliente dijo que el precio era demasiado alto. -> make it still-true exception with present.
Base: Laura pregunto si queria unirme a la llamada. -> change person to nosotros.

Ellos dijeron que fueron al concierto la noche anterior.
Marta dijo que hablo con su jefe.
Maria dijo que cuando estudiaba en la universidad, trabajaba en un cafe.
Me dijo que no escribiera mi nombre en la lista.
La jefa dijo que no usara el telefono en la reunion.
Miguel pregunto: "¿Cuando empieza la pelicula?"
Carmen pregunto: "¿Has estado en Madrid?"
Ana dice que necesita el archivo hoy.
Luis dijo que llamaria al cliente al dia siguiente.

contrast.instructions: Choose the natural indirect speech version.
prompts: She said she is tired (reported in past). (dijo que esta cansada vs dijo que estaba cansada)
He says he wants coffee (present reporting). (dice que queria cafe vs dice que quiere cafe)
She said: Close the door. (dijo que cierra la puerta vs dijo que cerrara la puerta)
He said he will finish tomorrow. (dijo que terminara manana vs dijo que terminaria manana)
They said they went yesterday. (dijeron que iban ayer vs dijeron que fueron ayer)
She said she has finished. (dijo que ha terminado vs dijo que habia terminado)
When does it start? (pregunto cuando empieza vs pregunto cuando empezaba)
Have you been to Madrid? (pregunto cuando has estado vs pregunto si habia estado)
María said she lives in Mexico (still true). (dijo que vivia en Mexico vs dijo que vive en Mexico)
He told me write your name. (me dijo que escribes vs me dijo que escribiera)
answers: Dijo que estaba cansada.
Dice que quiere cafe.
Dijo que cerrara la puerta.
Dijo que terminaria manana.
Dijeron que fueron ayer.
Dijo que habia terminado.
Pregunto cuando empezaba.
Pregunto si habia estado.
Dijo que vive en Mexico.
Me dijo que escribiera.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Required connector que is dropped like optional English that.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Sofia dijo que estaba muy cansada.

commonErrors.mistake: Carlos dijo que terminara el informe pronto
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Future backshift uses subjunctive instead of conditional.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Carlos dijo que terminaria el informe pronto.

commonErrors.mistake: Marta dijo que ha hablado con su jefe
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Present perfect is not shifted after past reporting verb.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Marta dijo que habia hablado con su jefe.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 80: Subjunctive by Clause Type
lessons.targetPattern: Identify the dependent clause type first (adverb, adjective, noun), then choose mood: time clauses use subjunctive for pending/future and indicative for habitual facts; adjective clauses use subjunctive for non-specific referents; noun clauses use subjunctive after doubt/emotion/influence and indicative after certainty.
lessons.englishTrap: Choosing subjunctive or indicative word-by-word without identifying what kind of dependent clause you are in.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish mood follows clause function: adverb clauses depend on timing (habitual vs pending), adjective clauses depend on reference type (known vs unknown), noun clauses depend on the main verb frame (WEIRDO/doubt vs certainty).
formula: adverb time + habitual/past: cuando/tan pronto como + indicative
adverb time + pending/future: cuando/hasta que/en cuanto + subjunctive
adverb always subjunctive: a menos que / antes de que / en caso de que
adjective + unknown referent: busco/necesito + noun + que + subjunctive
adjective + known referent: conozco + noun + que + indicative
noun + doubt/emotion: no creo/me molesta + que + subjunctive
noun + certainty: creo/es cierto/no dudo + que + indicative
naturalExamples: Siempre reviso el informe cuando llega el cliente.
Te llamo en cuanto pueda.
Busco un hotel que tenga buen wifi.
No creo que el precio sea justo.
Es cierto que el equipo trabaja mucho.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 79: Llegar Pattern Family
lessons.targetPattern: Use llegar a for arrival at places and eventual outcomes, llegar alone for received items and emotional impact, llegar a/hasta for levels and distance, llegar para for sufficiency, and fixed frames like llegar a un acuerdo.
lessons.englishTrap: Mapping every get/reach/receive/arrive to different English verbs instead of one Spanish frame family.
lessons.spanishLogic: Llegar covers arrival, reception, reaching levels, emotional impact, sufficiency, and agreement when paired with the right preposition or object pattern.
formula: arrive/get to place: llegar a + place (¿A que hora llegaste a la oficina?)
eventually/manage to: llegar a + infinitive (Llego a entenderlo.)
receive: me/nos llego/llegaron + thing (Me llegaron dos mensajes.)
reach level/amount: llegar a + number/milestone
extend distance: llegar hasta + point
enough: no me llega + para + infinitive
agreement/progress: llegar a un acuerdo / llegar tan lejos
naturalExamples: ¿A que hora llegaste a la oficina?
Me llegaron dos mensajes del cliente.
El informe llego a diez paginas.
Con el tiempo, llegaras a dominarlo.
No me llega el presupuesto para contratar a alguien mas.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 78: Pero, Sino, and Sino que
lessons.targetPattern: Use pero for general contrast between ideas; use sino to correct/replace a noun, adjective, or infinitive after negation; use sino que when the correction is a full clause with its own conjugated verb.
lessons.englishTrap: Using pero for every but, including not X but Y corrections.
lessons.spanishLogic: Pero adds contrast between coexisting ideas. Sino corrects or replaces what was negated (not this, but that). Sino que does the same when the replacement is an entire clause with a new verb.
formula: general contrast: idea + pero + contrasting idea
correct noun/adjective after no: No quiero X, sino Y.
correct infinitive after no: No quieren estudiar, sino dormir.
correct full clause after no: No compramos casa, sino que alquilamos.
simple quality contrast (no correction frame): es bueno, pero caro
naturalExamples: Este restaurante es bueno, pero es muy caro.
No quiero pizza, sino pasta.
No compramos una casa, sino que alquilamos un departamento.
Su perro no es negro, sino dorado.
Queremos reunirnos hoy, pero el cliente prefiere manana.

No quieren cancelar, sino posponer.

contrast.instructions: Choose the natural connector: pero, sino, or sino que.
prompts: Not pizza, but pasta. (No quiero pizza, pero pasta. vs No quiero pizza, sino pasta.)
Good but expensive. (Es bueno, sino caro. vs Es bueno, pero caro.)
We did not buy; we rented. (No compramos, sino alquilamos. vs No compramos, sino que alquilamos.)
Not black, but golden. (No es negro, pero dorado. vs No es negro, sino dorado.)
Not study, but sleep. (No quieren estudiar, pero dormir. vs No quieren estudiar, sino dormir.)
We want beach; they want shopping. (Queremos playa, sino que quieren compras. vs Queremos playa, pero quieren compras.)
Not angry, but tired. (No esta enojada, pero cansada. vs No esta enojada, sino cansada.)
I did not call; I emailed. (No llame, sino envie un correo. vs No llame, sino que envie un correo.)
Interesting but long. (Es interesante, sino larga. vs Es interesante, pero larga.)
Not the price, but the date. (No es el precio, pero la fecha. vs No es el precio, sino la fecha.)
answers: No quiero pizza, sino pasta.
Es bueno, pero caro.
No compramos, sino que alquilamos.
No es negro, sino dorado.
No quieren estudiar, sino dormir.
Queremos ir a la playa, pero quieren ir de compras.
No esta enojada, sino cansada.
No llame, sino que envie un correo.
Es interesante, pero larga.
No es el precio, sino la fecha.

prompts: The office is close but parking is impossible.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Pero is used for corrective replacement after negation.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No quiero pizza, sino pasta.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Sino is used for simple additive contrast without correction frame.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Es bueno, pero caro.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Full clause correction keeps sino without que.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No compramos, sino que alquilamos.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Corrective adjective replacement after negation uses pero.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No es negro, sino dorado.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Corrective infinitive after negation uses pero.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No quieren estudiar, sino dormir.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Two independent contrasting clauses are forced into sino frame.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Queremos ir a la playa, pero quieren ir de compras.

commonErrors.mistake: No llame, sino envie un correo
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Conjugated verb clause after negation lacks que.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No llame, sino que envie un correo.

commonErrors.mistake: No es el precio, pero la fecha
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Not-X-but-Y noun correction uses general contrast connector.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No es el precio, sino la fecha.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Sino que is omitted before second conjugated verb.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: No cancelamos, sino que posponemos.

commonErrors.mistake: Quiero el plan basico, pero no el premium
commonErrors.why_it_happens: Replacement noun after implicit negation should use sino.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Quiero el plan basico, no el premium / no quiero el premium, sino el basico.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 77: Accidental Se (Olvidársele, Caérsele, Rompérsele, Perdérsele, Acabársele)
sourceFiles: lessons/lesson-77/Traducciones con Olvidársele, Caérsele, Rompérsele, Perdérsele, y Acabársele.pdf
lessons.targetPattern: Frame unplanned loss, drops, breaks, forgetfulness, and running out with se + IO pronoun (me/te/le/nos/les) + verb; the affected thing is the grammatical subject and controls verb number.
lessons.englishTrap: Using I forgot / I dropped / I broke with the person as subject, as if the event were fully intentional.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish often externalizes the event: the thing happens to you. Se marks unplanned occurrence, the IO pronoun marks who is affected, and the lost/broken/forgotten item is the subject.
formula: forget: Se me olvido / Se me olvidaron + thing + (place)
drop: Se me cayo / Se me cayeron + thing
break: Se me rompio / Se nos rompieron + thing
lose/misplace: Se le perdio / Se me perdieron + thing + (place)
run out: Se me acabo / Se nos acabaron + thing
optional clarifier: A Maria se le perdio el pasaporte.
naturalExamples: Se me olvido el celular en casa.
Se le cayo la tarjeta de credito.
Se nos rompio la ventana.
A Maria se le perdio el pasaporte en el aeropuerto.
Se nos acabaron las excusas.

controlled.instructions: Write each sentence in Spanish using the accidental se + IO pronoun pattern.
prompts: I forgot my cellphone at home.

answers: Se me olvido el celular en casa.
Se le olvidaron las llaves en el trabajo.
Se te cayo la tarjeta de credito.
Se les cayeron las camaras.
Se nos rompio la ventana.
A Javier se le rompieron los audifonos.
A Maria se le perdio el pasaporte en el aeropuerto.
Se me perdieron los guantes.
Al equipo se le acabo el espacio en las maletas.
Se nos acabaron las excusas.
Se me olvido el enlace de la reunion en la oficina.
Se le cayo el telefono en las escaleras.
Se les rompio la laptop durante el viaje.
Se nos perdio el recibo del informe de gastos.
Se le acabo la bateria del telefono.
Se te olvido la credencial en casa.
Se les rompio el control remoto del proyector.
Se me cayeron las paginas del contrato en el pasillo.
A el cliente se le perdio la copia firmada en el hotel.
Se nos acabo el tiempo en la presentacion.

answers: Se me olvidaron las llaves en el trabajo.
Se les cayeron las camaras.

contrast.instructions: Choose the natural Spanish sentence for unplanned/affected-party framing.
prompts: I forgot my phone at home. (Olvide mi celular en casa. vs Se me olvido el celular en casa.)

They dropped the cameras. (Se les cayo las camaras. vs Se les cayeron las camaras.)
I fell down the stairs. (Se me cai en las escaleras. vs Me cai en las escaleras.)
I broke the phone on purpose. (Se me rompio el telefono. vs Rompi el telefono.)
Maria lost her passport. (A Maria le perdio el pasaporte. vs A Maria se le perdio el pasaporte.)
answers: Se me olvido el celular en casa.
Se me cayo la tarjeta.
Se nos rompio la ventana.
Se le perdio el pasaporte.
Se nos acabo el tiempo.
Se me olvidaron las llaves.
Se les cayeron las camaras.
Me cai en las escaleras.
Rompi el telefono.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: English puts the person as intentional subject instead of accidental se frame.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Active we-broke framing is used for an unplanned break.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Simple transitive lose replaces affected-party se pattern.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Singular verb with plural subject.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Se les cayeron las camaras.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Possessive is added even though affected item is already the subject.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Se me olvido el celular.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Accidental se is omitted before IO pronoun.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: A Maria se le perdio el pasaporte.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: Run-out event is expressed as direct transitive action.
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Se nos acabo el tiempo.

commonErrors.why_it_happens: IO pattern for thing dropping is confused with person falling (me cai).
commonErrors.correct_spanish: Se me cayo el telefono.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 76: Querer que + Subjunctive (Influence)
lessons.targetPattern: Use querer que + present subjunctive when one person wants another person to do something; keep subjects different and conjugate the subordinate verb in subjunctive.
lessons.englishTrap: Using indicative or infinitive after want when another person performs the action.
lessons.spanishLogic: Querer becomes an influence trigger when subjects differ: main clause states the desire, subordinate clause uses subjunctive for the other person's action.
formula: open question: ¿Que quieres que haga?
yes/no request frame: ¿Quieres que + subjunctive?
different subjects required: Quiero que tu llames / ¿Quieres que yo llame?
same subject -> infinitive: Quiero llamar (not quiero que llame for self)
object pronouns stay with subjunctive verb: ¿Quieres que te envie el archivo?
naturalExamples: ¿Que quieres que haga?
¿Quieres que llegue temprano a la reunion?
¿Quieres que organice los archivos?
¿Quieres que te envie el informe?
¿Quieres que llamemos al cliente?

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 75: Relative Pronoun Selection
lessons.targetPattern: Use que in restrictive clauses (never omit it); place prepositions before el/la que or quien; use lo que for ideas/clauses; choose quien/quienes for people in non-restrictive or after-preposition contexts.
lessons.englishTrap: Dropping the relative pronoun and leaving prepositions at the end of the clause.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish keeps an explicit connector (que, quien, el/la que, lo que) and puts the preposition before it. The connector type depends on whether the clause is restrictive, refers to people, or refers to an idea.
formula: restrictive default: noun + que + clause (El cliente que llamo ayer...)
never omit: El informe que revise (not El informe revise)
prep + relative: de la que / con el que / sobre la que
people after prep: a quien / con quienes
ideas/clauses: lo que + verb (Lo que necesito es tiempo.)
restrictive people: la persona que trabaja (not quien)
naturalExamples: El contrato que firmamos ayer ya esta activo.
La persona de la que hable esta en la sala.
Trabajo con un colega con quien comparto muchos proyectos.
Lo que necesito es una respuesta clara.
La candidata que recomendaste tiene mucha experiencia.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 74: Lo to Refer Back to Qualities and Actions
lessons.targetPattern: Use lo + ser/estar to refer back to adjective qualities (lo es/lo son/lo estan); use lo + hacer for manner or completed abstract actions; do not use lo for bare profession/nationality/religion answers unless the attribute is elaborated.
lessons.englishTrap: Repeating the full adjective or saying only si/no without a natural back-reference pronoun.
lessons.spanishLogic: Lo stands in for the already-mentioned quality or action. It stays invariable and pairs with ser, estar, or hacer depending on what you are referring back to.
formula: quality with ser: ¿Es interesante? -> Si, lo es. / No, no lo es.
plural quality: ¿Son caros? -> Si, lo son. / No, no lo son.
state with estar: ¿Estan listos? -> Si, lo estan.
manner/action: ¿Lo haces bien? -> Si, lo hago.
abstract completed action: Hizo buen trabajo. -> Si, lo hizo.
no lo for bare identity: ¿Es mexicana? -> Si, es.
naturalExamples: ¿Es interesante el informe? Si, lo es.
¿Son caros los vuelos? No, no lo son.
¿Estan listos los documentos? Si, lo estan.
¿Es mexicana? Si, es.
¿Es un buen candidato? Si, lo es.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 73: Adjective Position and Meaning Shifts
lessons.targetPattern: Place adjectives after the noun for objective/restrictive description; place them before (or use buen/mal/gran) when the adjective shifts meaning, adds subjective emphasis, or belongs to fixed pre-noun patterns (mucho, poco, ambos, ordinals).
lessons.englishTrap: Always putting adjectives after the noun in Spanish because that was the first classroom rule.
lessons.spanishLogic: Position is a meaning signal. After the noun = objective quality. Before the noun = subjective view, emphasis, or a different sense of the same adjective (nuevo, viejo, pobre, grande/gran, mismo, bueno/buen, malo/mal).
formula: restrictive/objective: noun + adjective (casa grande, coche nuevo)
subjective/emphasis: adjective + noun (hermosa ciudad, deliciosa cena)
meaning shift before: nuevo/viejo/antiguo/pobre/gran/mismo/unico/simple/diferente
bueno/malo before masculine singular: buen medico / mal resultado
fixed pre-noun: mucho/poco/ambos + noun; primero/segundo/tercero + noun
naturalExamples: Compre un nuevo coche, pero no es un coche nuevo.
Es un viejo amigo, no un amigo viejo.
Es un buen abogado, pero no un abogado bueno en lo personal.
Tenemos poco tiempo y mucho trabajo.
Vivimos en la misma calle, pero en casas diferentes.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 72: Definite Articles When English Omits Them
lessons.targetPattern: Use el/la/los/las for generic concepts, gustar categories, scheduled days, body parts, clothing, titles, streets, and languages as subjects; drop the article after ser + day, direct address, and hablar/aprender/saber + language.
lessons.englishTrap: Dropping articles wherever English drops them, especially with generics, body parts, clothing, and days of the week.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish marks whole categories and shared context with definite articles. If the noun refers to a general concept, a habitual day, or something clearly yours (body/clothing), use el/la/los/las instead of mirroring English zero-article patterns.
formula: generic/concept: La naturaleza / La salud / La democracia
gustar category: Me gusta la musica / Me gustan los perros
scheduled day: Nos vemos el lunes
ser + day (no article): Hoy es lunes
body/clothing (owner clear): Me duele la cabeza / Ponte el abrigo
title about someone: El doctor Garcia es...
direct address (no article): Doctor Garcia, ...
street: Vivo en la calle + name
language as subject: El espanol es util
language after hablar/aprender/saber: Hablo espanol
naturalExamples: La naturaleza es impresionante.
Me gusta la musica.
Nos vemos el lunes.
Me duele la cabeza.
Hablo espanol, pero el espanol es dificil.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 71: Imperfect Subjunctive in Past Narrative
lessons.targetPattern: Use imperfect subjunctive (-ra form) after the same triggers as present subjunctive in past narrative: reported commands/desires, emotion/hope in the past, pending time clauses, and si + conditional hypotheticals.
lessons.englishTrap: Keeping indicative forms in past subordinate clauses because English rarely marks mood this way.
lessons.spanishLogic: When the main clause is in the past and the trigger appears, shift the subordinate verb to imperfect subjunctive (-ra) to show influence, emotion, doubt, or pending action from that past viewpoint.
formula: past command/desire: dijo/queria/insistio en que + imperfect subjunctive
past hope/emotion: esperaba/me preocupaba que + imperfect subjunctive
pending time from past frame: en cuanto/antes de que + imperfect subjunctive
hypothetical: si + imperfect subjunctive, conditional
form: preterite ellos stem + -ra endings
naturalExamples: Juan me dijo que fuera a la tienda.
Ella esperaba que terminaramos a tiempo.
Me pidio que le llamara en cuanto tuviera la informacion.
Si fuera posible, me mudaria a otra ciudad.
Queria salir antes de que lloviera.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 70: Subjunctive vs Indicative Decisions
lessons.targetPattern: Choose subjunctive after uncertainty, negation, emotion, influence, and unknown-reference triggers; choose indicative for known facts and specific references.
lessons.englishTrap: Using one default present tense in every subordinate clause.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish mood depends on certainty and reference type: unknown/desired/emotional/pending contexts activate subjunctive, confirmed facts keep indicative.
formula: no creo/no pienso/dudo que + subjunctive
emotion/value: me alegra/me molesta/me preocupa que + subjunctive
influence/command: querer/preferir/insistir en que + subjunctive
unknown or non-specific referent: busco/necesito + noun + que + subjunctive
known/specific/factual statements: indicative
naturalExamples: No creo que ella tenga razon.
Me alegra que estes aqui.
Necesitamos un tecnico que sepa resolver esto.
Conozco un tecnico que sabe resolver esto.
Te llamo en cuanto pueda.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 69: Present Subjunctive Trigger Patterns
lessons.targetPattern: Use present subjunctive after key triggers like querer que, cuando for pending future actions, and antes de que; simplify to antes de + infinitive when the subject does not change.
lessons.englishTrap: Trying to translate clause by clause with indicative forms and skipping the trigger logic.
lessons.spanishLogic: Subjunctive is a pattern response: once the trigger appears, the next clause takes subjunctive if there is a subject change.
formula: querer que + subjunctive
avisame cuando + subjunctive (future/pending event)
antes de que + subjunctive
same subject: antes de + infinitive
build subjunctive from yo form base
naturalExamples: Quiero que llegues temprano manana.
Avisame cuando tengas la informacion.
Te llamo antes de que salgas.
Siempre cierro todo antes de acostarme.
Necesitamos que el equipo responda hoy.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 68: Conditional for Politeness and Hypotheticals
lessons.targetPattern: Use conditional forms to soften requests and invitations, express future-in-the-past in reported speech, and build hypotheticals with si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional.
lessons.englishTrap: Treating would as one direct translation and missing when Spanish uses conditional as a social softener.
lessons.spanishLogic: Conditional in Spanish is a function pattern: polite distance, projected action from past, and hypothetical consequence.
formula: polite request: podria/podrias + infinitive
offer/invitation: te/le gustaria + infinitive
reported speech: dijo que + conditional
hypothetical: si + imperfect subjunctive, conditional
conditional stem + ia endings across all verb groups
naturalExamples: Podria decirme la hora, por favor?
Te gustaria venir conmigo?
Ella dijo que llegaria mas tarde.
Si tuviera mas tiempo, estudiaria mas.
Si fueramos vecinos, nos veriamos mas.

contrast.instructions: Choose the most natural option for each context.
prompts: Polite formal request: Could you help me? (Podria ayudarme? vs Puedes ayudarme?)
Informal invitation: Would you like to come? (Te gustaria venir? vs Le gustaria venir?)
Reported promise from past: He said he would call. (Dijo que llamaria. vs Dijo que llama.)
Hypothetical unreal present: If I had time, I would go. (Si tuviera tiempo, iria. vs Si tengo tiempo, iria.)
Formal possibility phrasing: Would it be possible to talk now? (Seria posible hablar ahora? vs Es posible hablar ahora?)
Offer with social softening: Would you like coffee? (Le gustaria tomar cafe? vs Quieres cafe?)
Future-in-the-past with nosotros: We thought we would finish today. (Pensamos que terminariamos hoy. vs Pensamos que terminamos hoy.)
Hypothetical with you informal: If you studied more, you would pass. (Si estudiaras mas, pasarias. vs Si estudias mas, pasarias.)
Conditional ability: I would be able to go tomorrow. (Podria ir manana. vs Puedo ir manana.)
Hypothetical with ser: If she were the boss, she would change it. (Si ella fuera la jefa, lo cambiaria. vs Si ella era la jefa, lo cambiaria.)
answers: Podria ayudarme?
Te gustaria venir?
Dijo que llamaria.
Si tuviera tiempo, iria.
Seria posible hablar ahora?
Le gustaria tomar cafe?
Pensamos que terminariamos hoy.
Si estudiaras mas, pasarias.
Podria ir manana.
Si ella fuera la jefa, lo cambiaria.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 67: Preterite vs Imperfect with Meaning Shifts
lessons.targetPattern: Use preterite for bounded completed events, imperfect for background and habitual context, and apply tense-dependent meaning shifts in conocer, saber, querer, poder, and tener.
lessons.englishTrap: Choosing tense by translation only and missing how tense changes verb meaning.
lessons.spanishLogic: In narration, imperfect builds the scene and preterite advances events; some verbs switch meaning depending on tense.
formula: imperfect for background, repeated habits, states
preterite for completed event and timeline movement
interruption frame: imperfect action + preterite event
meaning shifts: conocer, saber, querer, poder, tener
continuous nuance: estaba + gerundio vs estuve + gerundio
naturalExamples: Cuando era nino, jugaba en ese parque.
Ayer conoci a la nueva gerente.
No sabia la respuesta hasta hoy.
Queria salir, pero no pude.
Estaba trabajando cuando sono el telefono.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 66: Por vs Para Decision Frames
lessons.targetPattern: Choose para for purpose, destination, deadlines, and intended recipient; choose por for cause, exchange, duration, means, movement through, and fixed chunks like gracias por.
lessons.englishTrap: Translating both por and para as for without checking function.
lessons.spanishLogic: Pick the preposition by meaning role: para points forward to goal or destination, por points backward to reason, method, exchange, or path.
formula: para + infinitive for purpose
para + person for recipient/benefit
para + date/time for deadline
por + noun for cause/reason
por + duration/means/path/exchange
naturalExamples: Estudio espanol para trabajar con clientes globales.
Sali para la oficina a las siete.
Necesito el informe para el viernes.
No fuimos por la lluvia.
Viajamos por tren y pagamos poco por los boletos.

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 65: Direct + Indirect Object Pronoun Stacking
lessons.targetPattern: Replace repeated nouns with direct and indirect object pronouns, keep IOP + DOP order, change le/les to se before lo/la/los/las, and place pronouns correctly with conjugated verbs, infinitives, and gerunds.
lessons.englishTrap: Keeping full nouns in every sentence or placing pronouns in English-like positions.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish sounds natural when you compress repeated objects into pronouns and follow a fixed stack: indirect first, direct second.
formula: single object replacement: lo/la/los/las
recipient replacement: me/te/le/nos/les
double pronouns: IOP + DOP
le/les + lo/la/los/las -> se + lo/la/los/las
placement: before conjugated verb or attached to infinitive/gerund
naturalExamples: Veo a Marta cada dia. La veo cada dia.
Le di el informe a Juan. Se lo di.
Te voy a mandar el archivo. Te lo voy a mandar.
Estoy escribiendo la carta para ella. Se la estoy escribiendo.
Puedes pasarme la sal? Puedes pasarmela?

lessons.title: Spanish Daily Lesson 64: Soler + Infinitive
lessons.targetPattern: Use soler plus infinitive to express present routines and past used-to habits with minimal conjugation load.
lessons.englishTrap: Overbuilding habitual meaning with adverbs and fully conjugated second verbs.
lessons.spanishLogic: Spanish compresses routine meaning through soler plus infinitive, with present forms for usually and imperfect forms for used to.
formula: present routine: suelo sueles suele solemos suelen plus infinitive
past routine: solia solias solia soliamos solian plus infinitive
main action stays infinitive after soler
subject pronoun often omitted in present
subject may be included in imperfect when context is ambiguous
naturalExamples: Suelo desayunar temprano.
Mi hermano suele levantarse a las seis.
Solemos cocinar en casa.
Solia leer todas las noches.
Mis padres solian viajar mucho.
